name: Fish and Chips
tagline: Beer-battered cod with twice-fried chips and malt vinegar
highlight: Beer batter shatters; chips crackle; no shellfish

hero.alt: Fish and chips with malt vinegar and lemon
hero.caption: fish & chips — beer battered

healthFacts: Cod is lean and mild — ~20g protein per fillet.
Twice-frying chips makes them crisp without oil-logging.

steps.title: Cut and parfry
steps.detail: Cut potatoes into 1/2-inch chips. Soak 15 min in cold water; drain and dry. Fry at 325°F for 5 min — they should be soft, not browned.
steps.tip: First fry softens; second fry crisps. Twice-frying is the secret.

steps.title: Whisk the batter
steps.detail: Whisk flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt. Just before frying, add cold beer; stir to combine — lumps OK.
steps.tip: Cold + carbonation = crisp. Don't make ahead.

steps.title: Crank to 375°F, fry chips
steps.detail: Bring oil to 375°F. Fry chips a second time 3 min until deep gold and crisp. Drain on rack, salt while hot.
steps.tip: Listen for the sound — quiet frying = limp; loud = crisp.

steps.title: Batter and fry fish
steps.detail: Pat fish dry, dip in batter, lower into oil. Fry 4–5 min, turning once, until deep gold. Drain. Serve with chips, lemon, malt vinegar.
steps.tip: Hold the fish at the surface 5 sec before letting go — sets the crust so it doesn't stick to the bottom.
